_description: Internal reference dataset derived from user-provided SeaOps Solutions CTV workability material for Kincardine. It preserves traceability for future vessel-operability methodology design and must not be used as GTM, client, project, or design-basis material without explicit user approval, source-rights review, and a separate approved issue.

_source.title: Month-by-month CTV operability as-built [N] only vs [N] + [S]
_source.license_note: Internal reference retention only. No external use, publication, client reuse, or marketing reuse until source rights are resolved and the user explicitly approves a separately planned issue. Preserve attribution to SeaOps Solutions in internal records.

_references: SeaOps Solutions infographic: CTV Workability · Kincardine — Month-by-month CTV operability as-built [N] only vs [N]+[S]
NORA3 hindcast / climatology basis stated in source graphic: 56.95°N, 1.87°W; 1979-2025; 411,264 hours

usage_boundary.competitor_context: SeaOps Solutions is competitor-adjacent reference material; do not copy wording, layout, claims, or digitized values into deliverables.
usage_boundary.promotion_rule: Project, client, or marketing reuse requires explicit user approval, source-rights review, and a separate approved issue.

data_confidence.source_native_requirement: Regenerate from licensed or public metocean and project data before engineering or external use.

methodology_boundary.reference_observation: Use only to study high-level operability-reporting concepts such as monthly access windows, configuration sensitivity, daylight/environmental constraints, and bottleneck-month identification.
methodology_boundary.design_basis_engineering: Requires source-native metocean data, vessel/access limits, landing geometry, validated criteria, provenance, uncertainty handling, and project-specific review before any engineering decision.
methodology_boundary.original_work_requirement: Future AceEngineer/digitalmodel outputs must use original wording, original visual hierarchy, verified source data, and explicit provenance rather than copying competitor material.

constraints.wave_constraint_note: Source graphic states Ws <= 12 m/s; preserve original notation pending source clarification.

monthly_operability_climatology.note: Winter/daylight-bound; source callout reports Jan 6.8% with [S].
Peak absolute uplift; source callout rounds 28% to 43% (+15.3 pp).
Winter/daylight-bound; source callout reports Dec 6.2% with [S].

reference_insights.primary_observation: A second landing direction may materially change access-window availability and reveal monthly bottlenecks that static vessel specifications do not expose.
internal_analysis_questions: How should access mode and landing direction be represented in a source-native data model?
Which monthly and seasonal operability summaries are useful for engineering triage?
Which daylight, wind, wave, and transfer criteria must be explicit before design-basis use?
How should future reports separate observation, provenance, confidence class, and engineering caveat language?
engineering_caveats: Monthly bar values are digitized or approximated from the supplied infographic; use source data for engineering decisions.
Annual headline metrics are source-stated and should be treated as more authoritative than approximate chart digitization.
This reference supports internal data-shape design only and is not a substitute for site-specific marine operations analysis.
